name: United States–Mexico–Canada Agreement
climate: Arctic and subarctic in northern Canada, temperate continental, subtropical in southern US, tropical in southern Mexico, arid deserts in the southwest

neighbors.name: European Union
neighbors.relevance: Largest transatlantic trade partner; US-EU trade and investment relationship is the world's largest bilateral

neighbors.name: CPTPP
neighbors.relevance: Canada and Mexico are CPTPP members; overlapping Pacific trade commitments

neighbors.name: Mercosur
neighbors.relevance: Key Latin American trade partner; agricultural and energy trade flows north-south

neighbors.name: Indo-Pacific
neighbors.relevance: US Indo-Pacific strategy drives trade policy; Pacific coast ports handle Asian trade

neighbors.name: Five Eyes
neighbors.relevance: USA and Canada are Five Eyes members; intelligence sharing underpins security cooperation

neighbors.name: G7
neighbors.relevance: USA and Canada are G7 members; coordinate on trade, sanctions, and economic policy

links.description: Corn Belt is within USMCA territory; US grain exports to Mexico are a core trade flow
Rust Belt manufacturing is deeply integrated into US-Canada-Mexico auto supply chains
Silicon Valley tech sector drives USMCA digital trade and IP provisions
Panama Canal connects US Gulf and East Coast to Pacific markets; critical for USMCA trade
Canada and Mexico in both blocs; creates overlapping trade rules for Pacific commerce
Transatlantic trade exceeds $1 trillion annually; regulatory alignment and divergence shape markets
All three USMCA members are G20 members; coordinate on global economic governance
